metadata.description: Kanban board for Azure AD B2C Authentication Integration - Login/Logout. Contains original epic task (as reference) and broken-down subtasks (1-2 day units). See ../KANBAN-BREAKDOWN-GUIDE.md for breakdown details.
metadata.epicTitle: Azure AD B2C Authentication Integration - Login/Logout
metadata.note: This file contains the original epic task and 6 broken-down subtasks (1-2 day units).

tasks.title: Azure AD B2C Authentication Integration - Login/Logout
tasks.description: Integrate Azure AD B2C for secure authentication including login, logout, session management, and 'Remember me' functionality.
tasks.userStory: As a store owner or employee, I want to securely log in and log out of the system so that I can access the features I'm authorized to use while maintaining security.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user visits the login page, when they enter valid credentials, then they are authenticated and redirected to the dashboard
Given a user attempts to log in, when credentials are invalid, then they receive an error message without revealing which field is incorrect
Given a user successfully logs in, when they select 'Remember me', then their session persists across browser sessions
Given a logged-in user, when they click logout, then their session is terminated and they are redirected to the login page
Given a user with an active session, when they navigate to protected routes, then they can access those routes without re-authentication
Given a user with an expired session, when they attempt to access protected routes, then they are redirected to login with a message about session expiration
tasks.businessValue: Provides secure authentication foundation required for all user interactions. Ensures data security and user access control.
tasks.technicalNotes: Requires Azure AD B2C tenant setup, custom user flows for sign-in, sign-up, password reset. Frontend must handle OAuth2/OIDC flow.
successMetrics: Login success rate: 95%+ of login attempts succeed
Session security: Zero unauthorized access incidents
businessRules: Sessions expire after 24 hours of inactivity (or 7 days with 'Remember me')
Failed login attempts are rate-limited (5 attempts per 15 minutes)
Session tokens must be securely stored
tasks.note: This is the original epic task. The subtasks below break this down into 1-2 day units.

tasks.title: Azure AD B2C Tenant Setup and Configuration
tasks.description: Set up Azure AD B2C tenant, configure user flows for sign-up, sign-in, and password reset.
tasks.userStory: As a developer, I want to configure Azure AD B2C so that authentication infrastructure is ready.
acceptanceCriteria: Given Azure subscription, when B2C tenant is created, then tenant is configured with appropriate settings
Given user flows, when configured, then sign-up, sign-in, and password reset flows are created
Given application registration, when app is registered, then client ID and secrets are obtained
Given redirect URIs, when configured, then production and development URIs are set
Given configuration, when complete, then settings are documented and stored securely
tasks.technicalNotes: Create Azure AD B2C tenant. Configure user flows. Register application. Document configuration.
successMetrics: All user flows are configured correctly
businessRules: B2C tenant must be in same region as app
Application secrets stored in Azure Key Vault

tasks.title: Backend Authentication Middleware
tasks.description: Implement authentication middleware in Azure Functions to validate JWT tokens from Azure AD B2C.
tasks.userStory: As a developer, I want authentication middleware so that protected endpoints can verify user identity.
acceptanceCriteria: Given HTTP request, when token is present, then token is validated against Azure AD B2C
Given valid token, when validated, then user claims are extracted and made available to function
Given invalid token, when detected, then 401 Unauthorized response is returned
Given missing token, when required endpoint is accessed, then 401 Unauthorized is returned
Given token validation, when successful, then CompanyId is extracted from token claims
Given middleware, when applied, then it works with all protected endpoints
tasks.technicalNotes: Use Microsoft.Identity.Web or similar library. Validate token signature and claims. Extract user info.
businessRules: Tokens must be from configured B2C tenant

tasks.title: Frontend Login Page and OAuth Flow
tasks.description: Create React login page that integrates with Azure AD B2C OAuth2/OIDC flow for user authentication.
tasks.userStory: As a developer, I want a login page so that users can authenticate using Azure AD B2C.
acceptanceCriteria: Given login page, when accessed, then it displays email and password fields with login button
Given user clicks login, when credentials are entered, then OAuth flow is initiated with Azure AD B2C
Given OAuth flow, when user authenticates, then redirect returns to app with authorization code
Given authorization code, when received, then app exchanges code for tokens
Given successful login, when tokens are obtained, then user is redirected to dashboard
Given login error, when authentication fails, then error message is displayed to user
tasks.technicalNotes: Use @azure/msal-react or similar. Implement OAuth2/OIDC flow. Handle redirects. Store tokens securely.
businessRules: Tokens stored in secure storage (not localStorage)
OAuth redirect URIs must match B2C configuration

tasks.title: Session Management and Token Storage
tasks.description: Implement secure token storage and session management including token refresh and expiration handling.
tasks.userStory: As a developer, I want secure session management so that users stay logged in appropriately.
acceptanceCriteria: Given user login, when tokens are obtained, then they are stored securely (sessionStorage or httpOnly cookies)
Given token expiration, when token expires, then refresh token is used to obtain new access token
Given session, when user is inactive for 24 hours, then session expires and user must re-authenticate
Given 'Remember me', when selected, then session persists for 7 days
Given token refresh, when refresh fails, then user is logged out and redirected to login
Given session management, when implemented, then it works across page refreshes
tasks.businessValue: Ensures secure and user-friendly session management.
tasks.technicalNotes: Implement token refresh logic. Use secure storage. Handle token expiration. Implement session timeout.
successMetrics: Token refresh succeeds 95%+ of the time
Sessions expire correctly based on inactivity
businessRules: Sessions expire after 24h inactivity (or 7d with Remember Me)

tasks.title: Protected Routes and Route Guards
tasks.description: Implement React route guards that check authentication status and redirect unauthenticated users to login.
tasks.userStory: As a developer, I want route guards so that protected pages require authentication.
acceptanceCriteria: Given protected route, when accessed by authenticated user, then route is accessible
Given protected route, when accessed by unauthenticated user, then user is redirected to login
Given login redirect, when user logs in, then they are redirected back to originally requested page
Given route guard, when token is expired, then user is redirected to login
Given route guard, when implemented, then it works for all protected routes
Given public routes, when accessed, then they are accessible without authentication
tasks.businessValue: Protects application routes from unauthorized access.
tasks.technicalNotes: Create ProtectedRoute component. Check authentication status. Handle redirects. Store original destination.
businessRules: All routes except login/signup are protected
Original destination is preserved after login

tasks.title: Logout Functionality
tasks.description: Implement logout functionality that clears session, invalidates tokens, and redirects to login.
tasks.userStory: As a user, I want to log out so that my session is terminated and I can securely end my session.
acceptanceCriteria: Given logged-in user, when logout is clicked, then session is cleared and tokens are removed
Given logout, when processed, then user is redirected to login page
Given logout, when successful, then Azure AD B2C session is also terminated (if possible)
Given logout, when completed, then user cannot access protected routes without re-authenticating
tasks.technicalNotes: Clear token storage. Call B2C logout endpoint if available. Clear application state. Redirect to login.
businessRules: All tokens must be cleared on logout
